name: Shapash
description: Shapash, also known as Shapshu, is the Canaanite goddess of the sun. Her name literally translates to 'Sun' or 'Torch.' She is a powerful and influential figure in Canaanite mythology, often depicted as a radiant and illuminating force. Shapash is not just a celestial body, but an active participant in the affairs of gods and mortals, wielding significant authority. She is associated with warmth, light, healing, and the revelation of truth. Shapash is often invoked for her ability to expose hidden things and to bring justice to light. Her role extends to the underworld, where she illuminates the realm of the dead, guiding souls and bearing witness to their fate. She possesses the power to influence life and death, and her pronouncements are considered final and unchangeable. In the Baal Cycle, Shapash plays a crucial role, particularly in the events surrounding Baal's death and resurrection. She intervenes to locate Baal's body and mourns his demise. Her grief is said to cause a drought on earth. Shapash also acts as a messenger and mediator between the gods, conveying information and resolving disputes. She is a vital link between the divine and mortal realms, and her presence is essential for maintaining cosmic order.
subtitle: Goddess of the Sun and Truth

keyMyths.title: Shapash and Baal's Death
keyMyths.description: When Baal is killed by Mot, Shapash searches for his body and mourns his death, causing a drought. Her grief demonstrates her power and influence over the natural world.

keyMyths.title: Shapash as Messenger
keyMyths.description: Shapash acts as a messenger between the gods, conveying important information and resolving disputes. Her words carry weight and are often considered final.

keyMyths.title: Shapash in the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Shapash illuminates the underworld, guiding the souls of the dead. She witnesses the fate of mortals and plays a role in the transition from life to death.

sources.author: Mark S. Smith
sources.work: The Ugaritic Baal Cycle, Volume I: Introduction with Text, Translation and Commentary
sources.description: A comprehensive analysis of the Baal Cycle, including Shapash's role.
